100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 9, 1926, Britain announced plans to increase its naval strength amid rising tensions in international waters. In the United States, the Senate continued debates over the controversial McNary-Haugen bill, aimed at supporting struggling farmers through federal intervention. Meanwhile, in the realm of aviation, famed pilot Charles Lindbergh expressed intentions to undertake new transatlantic flights, sparking excitement in the aviation community.
